title: Review the repository for regression risks around adding MBP setup for /kernel and /vote Codex prompts. Focus on existing tests like tests/test-kernel-launcher*.sh, tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh, tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, tests/test-codex-vote-prompt.sh, and README/CODEX prompt contract....
summary_text: 1. **High**: MBP `/kernel` launcher path is effectively unguarded in CI. The gate runs only prompt/static checks (`bash scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh`, `bash tests/test-codex-vote-prompt.sh`) and does not execute launcher/entrypoint tests (`test-kernel-launcher*.sh`, `test-kernel-entrypoint.sh`, `test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh`). Regressions in `kernel`/`codex` MBP setup can merge undetected. [.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:240](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:240) [.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:245](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml:245) 2. **High**: The MBP-specific `/kernel` specialist rule exists only in prompt text and is not asserted by the kernel prompt contract check. Prompt says “on MBP prefer `copilot-cli` when `gemini-cli` reviewer lanes are unhealthy,” but the checker only validates generic dynamic selection text. This is a doc-only contract risk. [kernel.md:96](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:96) [k.md:98](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/k.md:98) [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:92](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:92) 3. **Medium**: Contract/behavior mismatch risk for guard authority. Docs require local adapter path to route through `codex-kernel-guard launch`, but launcher behavior/tests intentionally prefer `k new` before guard in non-interactive fallback. This can produce divergent expectations during MBP setup changes. [CODEX.md:95](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md:95) [test-kernel-launcher-template.sh:74](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh:74) [test-kernel-launcher-template.sh:85](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh:85) 4. **Medium**: `test-kernel-launcher.sh` is non-hermetic and can validate the wrong binary. It binds to whatever `kernel` is on `PATH` instead of the repo launcher, so stale local install state can mask regressions in current source. [test-kernel-launcher.sh:8](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh:8) [test-kernel-launcher.sh:9](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh:9) 5. **Low**: README `/k` prompt path is machine-specific absolute path (`/Users/masayuki_otawara/...`), while the static check only verifies a substring. Broken contract links can pass tests and confuse MBP setup users. [README.md:566](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:566) [check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:262](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:262)
